sections.title: Key design principles – simple, asymmetric and dynamic

columns.title: Simple
columns.content:
### Simplicity of form

Basic geometric shapes inform the design of our furniture and signage.

### Simplicity of colour

Complementary colour combinations. Not too many colours used at once.

### Simplicity of pattern

Wallpaper and upholstery patterns are geometric.

columns.title: Asymmetric
columns.content:
### Asymmetric arrangement

We position our furniture in interesting and unexpected groupings. Asymmetric placement of carpets can help to create zones, or ‘rooms within a room’.

### Asymmetric color balance

Mix dark/neutral furniture with small spots of colored furniture to achieve an asymmetric balance of color.

columns.title: Dynamic
columns.content:
### Dynamic use of space

Create flexible spaces with multiple usages.

### Dynamic use of color

Use bold accents of color to create visual interest and define spaces.
